Title: Manectite

The Manectite (Japanese: ライボルトナイト Livoltnite) is a type of held item introduced in Generation VI. It is the Mega Stone that allows Manectric to Mega Evolve into Mega Manectric. If held by a Manectric, the Manectite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Manectric if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Manectite from Manectric. If held by a Manectric, the Manectite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Manectric when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Manectric will remain a Mega Manectric until the Talent Round ends. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Manectite allows the player to Mega Evolve Manectric. The player can obtain the Manectite by completing the competitive stage available in Week 7 of the event rotation. The Manectite was also available as a prize during the Mega Manectric competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Manectite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Manectite first appeared in Fast-Thinking Froakie, where Clemont helped X and his friends find it at Route 5. Unfortunately, a Team Flare Grunt tasked in collecting Mega Stones had also found the stone, and escaped with it. X, Trevor, and a Manectric, who had gotten the group into searching the stone to begin with, chased the Grunt to Parfum Palace, where they eventually managed to get the stone back. The Manectric, which had joined X's party with the nickname "Élec" during the chase, subsequently used the stone to Mega Evolve and send the Grunt running away. Élec would then start wearing the Manectite in his mane, allowing him to Mega Evolve whenever it is necessary. The Manectite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Garchompite

The Garchompite (Japanese: ガブリアスナイト Gaburiasnite) is a type of held item introduced in Generation VI. It is the Mega Stone that allows Garchomp to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp. If held by a Garchomp, the Garchompite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Garchompite from Garchomp. If held by a Garchomp, the Garchompite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Garchomp will remain a Mega Garchomp until the Talent Round ends. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Garchompite allows the player to Mega Evolve Garchomp. The player can obtain the Garchompite by completing the competitive stage available in Week 3 of the event rotation. The Garchompite was also available as a prize during the Mega Garchomp competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Garchompite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Garchompite was first seen in Mega Evolution Special I, belonging to Remo. His Garchomp, Garchoo, wears the Mega Stone around his neck. The same Garchompite made a brief reappearance in Analysis Versus Passion!. A Garchompite belonging to Professor Sycamore and his Garchomp debuted in Garchomp's Mega Bond!. Garchomp wears her Mega Stone as a choker around her neck. In The Fiery Road to Mastership!, Cynthia was revealed to own a Garchompite, which her Garchomp uses to Mega Evolve. Pocket Monsters XY: The Legend of the Pokémon Dragon King Koji owns a Garchompite, allowing his Garchomp to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp. The Garchompite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Roseli Berry

A Roseli Berry (Japanese: ロゼルのみ Rozelle Fruit) is a type of Berry introduced in Generation VI. If the holder is hit by a supereffective Fairy-type attack, it will consume its held Roseli Berry and halve the attack's damage. If the holder has Ripen, the damage is multiplied by 1/4. The Roseli Berry's design and name may be based on a roselle.

Title: Kee Berry

A Kee Berry (Japanese: アッキのみ Acki Fruit) is a type of Berry introduced in Generation VI. A Kee Berry raises the holder's Defense by one stage when hit by a physical move. This consumes the Kee Berry. If the holder has Ripen, then its Defense rises by two stages. The Kee Berry's design and name may be based on an ackee.

Title: Maranga Berry

A Maranga Berry (Japanese: タラプのみ Tarapu Fruit) is a type of Berry introduced in Generation VI. A Maranga Berry raises the holder's Special Defense by one stage when hit by a special move. This consumes the Maranga Berry. If the holder has Ripen, then its Special Defense rises by two stages. A Maranga Berry has been featured on the Tropical Party card sleeves. Excluding Berries with adjectives in their name, it has the longest name of a Berry in English, with seven letters. The Maranga Berry's design and name may be based on a marang.

Title: Discount Coupon

The Discount Coupon (Japanese: バーゲンチケット Bargain Ticket) is an item introduced in Generation VI. It can be exchanged at any boutique for a 50% discount on a single transaction. If the boutique fitting room is used to purchase multiple clothing items at once, using the coupon will apply the discount to all of the selected items.

Title: Strange Souvenir

The Strange Souvenir (Japanese: ふしぎなおきもの Mysterious Ornament) is a valuable item introduced in Generation VI. In Pokémon X and Y, the player is given the Strange Souvenir by a mysterious NPC who hints it is from a then-unrevealed region. This region was later revealed to be Alola. The Strange Souvenir serves no practical purpose other than to be sold.

Title: Lumiose Galette

The Lumiose Galette (Japanese: ミアレガレット Miare Galette) is a type of medicine introduced in Generation VI. It is a local specialty of Lumiose City and cures a Pokémon from all non-volatile status conditions and confusion. In Pokémon X and Y, the Lumiose Galette costs $80 if the player has at least 50 points of style. When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it cures a Pokémon of all non-volatile status conditions (poison, paralysis, sleep, burn, freeze) and confusion. In Pokémon: Magikarp Jump, Lumiose Galettes can appear as food that can be consumed by Magikarp. They are unlocked at Trainer Rank 54 and cost 30,428 to unlock. Lumiose Galettes first appeared in Kalos League Passion with a Certain Flare!, where Meyer and Clembot brought Ash, his friends, Sawyer, and their Pokémon two boxes of them to celebrate Ash advancing to the finals of the Lumiose Conference. More Lumiose Galettes appeared in The First Day of the Rest of Your Life!, where Ash and Serena bought themselves some to eat from a bakery in Lumiose City. In Turning Heads and Training Hard!, Ilima was seen serving some Lumiose Galettes that he had brought from Kalos to Professor Kukui and Samson Oak. In Battle Three With Bea!, Korrina mentioned that Bea really likes Lumiose Galettes, but she had been unable to buy any for her. In The Future is Now, Thanks to Strategy!, Bonnie brought Ash some Lumiose Galettes during his and Goh's visit to the Lumiose Gym In It's... Champion Time!, James's Morpeko was seen eating a Lumiose Galette. Lumiose Galette was introduced as an Item card in the Pokémon Trading Card Game during the English Mega Evolution Series (the Japanese MEGA Series). It was first released in the Japanese Nihil Zero expansion and the English Perfect Order expansion, with artwork by 5ban Graphics. The Lumiose Galette card heals 20 damage and removes one Special Condition (the equivalent of status conditions in the core series games) from the player's Active Pokémon. This effect is identical to the previously released Big Malasada card from the Sun & Moon expansion, which depicts another local specialty that heals status conditions. Lumiose Galettes are also seen in the illustrations of Hoopa from the Forbidden Light expansion and the Full Art illustration rare print of Morpeko from the Paradox Rift expansion. The Lumiose Galette has been featured in several officially licensed figures and goods.

Title: Jaw Fossil

The Jaw Fossil (Japanese: アゴのカセキ Jaw Fossil) is a fossil introduced in Generation VI that can be regenerated into Tyrunt. It is the counterpart of the Sail Fossil. If brought to the one of the following locations, the Jaw Fossil can be regenerated into a Tyrunt. The Jaw Fossil was introduced as an Item card in the Pokémon Trading Card Game in the Furious Fists expansion, first released in the Japanese Rising Fist expansion. It allows players to look at the bottom seven cards of their deck, reveal a Tyrunt they find there, and put it onto their Bench. The other six cards are then shuffled back into the deck. Main article: Antique Jaw Fossil (Perfect Order 68) The Mega Evolution Series saw the release of Antique Jaw Fossil in the Perfect Order expansion, first released in the Japanese Nihil Zero expansion. This card is unlike the previous Jaw Fossil card and more in line with other Item cards depicting Fossils, allowing the player to play it onto the Bench as if it were a Colorless-type Basic Pokémon. It has an Ability that reduces damages from attacks used by the opponent's Active Pokémon by 30. Unidentified Fossil cards are Fossil Trainer cards which become Basic Pokémon. While they do not feature a Jaw Fossil, they can still be used to evolve into certain Tyrunt. The Jaw Fossil has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Sail Fossil

The Sail Fossil (Japanese: ヒレのカセキ Fin Fossil) is a fossil introduced in Generation VI that can be regenerated into Amaura. It is the counterpart of the Jaw Fossil. If brought to the one of the following locations, the Sail Fossil can be regenerated into an Amaura. A Sail Fossil made a brief appearance in a flashback in Coming Back into the Cold!. It was discovered from a glacier by a group of researchers from the Ambrette Town Fossil Lab, and it was eventually revived into two Amaura, one of which later evolved into an Aurorus. The Sail Fossil was introduced as an Item card in the Pokémon Trading Card Game in the Furious Fists expansion, first released in the Japanese Rising Fist expansion. It allows players to look at the bottom seven cards of their deck, reveal an Amaura they find there, and put it onto their Bench. The other six cards are then shuffled back into the deck. Main article: Antique Sail Fossil (Perfect Order 69) The Mega Evolution Series saw the release of Antique Sail Fossil in the Perfect Order expansion, first released in the Japanese Nihil Zero expansion. This card is unlike the previous Sail Fossil card and more in line with other Item cards depicting Fossils, allowing the player to play it onto the Bench as if it were a Colorless-type Basic Pokémon. It has an Ability that prevents all effects of Supporter cards played from the opponent's hand done to it. A Sail Fossil is also featured on Unidentified Fossil cards. These cards are played as if they were Basic Pokémon and can evolve into Amaura. The Sail Fossil has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Fairy Gem

The Fairy Gem (Japanese: フェアリージュエル Fairy Jewel) is a type of held item that first appeared in game data in Generation VI. It is one of the Gems. It cannot be obtained in any game. When held by a Pokémon, it increases the power of the first Fairy-type move used by 30%, and is consumed after use. Fling will fail if the user is holding a Fairy Gem. The Fairy Gem has not been obtainable in any game.

Title: Elevator Key

If you were looking for the key in Pokémon Colosseum and XD, see Elevator Key (Orre). The Elevator Key (Japanese: エレベーターのキー Elevator Key) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It unlocks the elevator in Lysandre Labs. The Elevator Key unlocks the elevator in Lysandre Labs, allowing the player to access its lower floors. Lab Key Card A, Lab Key Card B, Lab Key Card C, Lab Key Card M, and Lab Key Card X

Title: Holo Caster

This article is about the item in Generation VI. For the segment at the end of Japanese episodes of Pokémon the Series: XY, see Professor Oak's Pokémon Holo Caster. The Holo Caster (Japanese: ホロキャスター Holo Caster) is a Key Item in Generation VI, introduced in Pokémon X and Y. It is a device developed by Lysandre Labs that allows people to communicate with each other through holograms. The player automatically starts their game with a Holo Caster. Its color depends on the player's gender: green for male or red for female. At various points in the game's plot, the player will receive messages from their friends and allies to advance the story. It is revealed later in the storyline that Team Flare spied on messages sent by the Holo Caster, although Sina and Dexio destroy the machine which recorded the messages shortly after Team Flare is defeated in Geosenge Town. By accessing the Holo Caster via PSS, it was used to receive special news bulletins about events and tournaments, and receive special distributions from participating stores and event locations via SpotPass. With StreetPass, the player can collect reports on other players as they pass by them. These are presented in a similar way to a news report, with the reporter being Malva. The Holo Caster service was discontinued in October 2016 in preparation for the release of Pokémon Sun and Moon. In the Looker Bureau missions, the Holo Caster is frequently used by Emma, Malva, and Looker. In any mode, the Nintendo 3DS's internal gyroscope can be used to move the background behind the character on-screen. The SpotPass and StreetPass functions of the Holo Caster are accessible through the PSS section of the PlayNav, even though the device itself is not obtainable in the game. The Holo Caster is no longer available as a key item in Legends: Z-A, but is referenced by other non-player characters. It is now able to project full life-sized holograms of people, and this function is used regularly by Tarragon and Jacinthe. Tarragon uses the Holo Caster to masquerade as Canari for certain in-person events such as the Canari Quiz Whiz Contest, and controls the movements of this hologram by using a separate app. Jacinthe's hologram is uniquely purple (while other holograms are blue), and this hologram is capable of battling and Mega Evolving her Pokémon. In Pokémon the Series, Holo Casters have primarily appeared under the ownership of characters associated with Team Flare. The first one was first seen in Mega Evolution Special I, under the ownership of Alain, while another Holo Caster appeared in Mega Evolution Special II, this time under the ownership of Lysandre. The Team Flare Scientists also possess a Holo Caster each, as first seen in From A to Z!. The Holo Caster was featured in The Beauty Eternal, in which Lysandre announced it during a press conference he held in Lumiose City. It was introduced as a product that would bring Kalosian people closer together. The Holo Caster became a huge success across Kalos and Lysandre Labs decided to increase its production due to excess demand. With what he gained from the Holo Caster, Lysandre was able to donate medical equipment to a Pokémon Center. Trevor owns a Holo Caster, which was first shown in An X-cuse to Come Out and Play. He frequently uses it to contact Professor Sycamore. It was broken during the battle between Xerneas and Yveltal, but was later repaired by Lysandre.

Title: Intriguing Stone

The Intriguing Stone (Japanese: すごそうないし Precious-looking Stone) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is an unidentified Mega Stone, whose identity is revealed in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. The Intriguing Stone can be exchanged with a Hiker in Shalour City for a Sun Stone after it has been shown to Gurkinn in the Tower of Mastery. The Intriguing Stone can be taken to the Devon Corporation in Rustboro City to be shown to Mr. Stone, who identifies it as a Pidgeotite.

Title: Lens Case

The Lens Case (Japanese: レンズケース Lens Case) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It allows the player to change the color of their eyes. The Lens Case stores colored contact lenses, which allow the player to change their eye color in fitting rooms. The player can choose from the following contacts when changing their contact lenses. In Pokémon X and Y, the player does not obtain contacts matching their default eye color; in Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon, the player is wearing colored contacts by default.

Title: Looker Ticket

The Looker Tickets (Japanese: ハンサムチケット Handsome Tickets) are Key Items introduced in Generation VI. They are a set of five tickets that Looker tasks the player to find to test them during the Looker Bureau post-game quest. Collecting the five Looker Tickets that Looker hid throughout Lumiose City will allow the player to progress the storyline. In his sync pair story in Pokémon Masters EX, Looker is revealed to have introduced Pasio to his concept of searching for Looker Tickets to help people improve their detective skills. He rewards anyone who manages to collect all five tickets with a restaurant coupon.

Title: Mega Ring

A Mega Ring (Japanese: メガリング Mega Ring) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is a Key Stone accessory that enables Mega Evolution. Its counterpart in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire is the Mega Bracelet. At the Tower of Mastery, Gurkinn offers a single Mega Ring to the player and their friends, but due to only having a single Mega Ring to give away, requests that they decide among themselves who should receive it. Of the group, only the player and Serena/Calem are interested in receiving it, so the two battle to decide who should receive it. Upon defeating Serena/Calem, the player must obtain the Rumble Badge at the Shalour Gym and then go to the top of the Tower of Mastery to receive the ring from Korrina. After the player has defeated their rival in Kiloude City once, Professor Sycamore will upgrade the player's Mega Ring at the Anistar Sundial by having the player touch the sundial. Once upgraded, the player can then find hidden Mega Stones around Kalos from 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM. AZ is rumored to have traveled to Hoenn, where he researched the origins of Mega Evolution to satisfy his own curiosity, then taught Quasartico Inc. how to make Mega Rings with his research. When Ange awakened within Prism Tower, it started unleading Mega Power, causing wild Pokémon to undergo Rogue Mega Evolution. To stop the tower, Quasartico planned to find the strongest Mega Evolution user by providing certain Trainers of the Z-A Royale from Rank G and above with Mega Rings in order for them to use Mega Evolution. In Pokémon X and Y, after the player has visited Kiloude City and participated in a battle at the Battle Maison, Calem/Serena will battle the player once per day at the northern part of the city, having obtained their own Mega Ring. In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, Courtney/Matt use Mega Rings during the Delta Episode, although they are not seen on their artwork. In Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon, Sina and Dexio use white Mega Rings, which they each wear around their left wrist, though only Dexio has the opportunity to use it outside the Battle Tree. In Pokémon Legends: Z-A, Urbain/Taunie, Naveen, and Lida each have a Mega Ring. Allows the player to Mega Evolve their Pokémon if they hold the appropriate Mega Stone. Each Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle. In Mega Evolution Special I, Alain was seen wearing a Mega Ring, which he uses to Mega Evolve his Charizard into Mega Charizard X. In Mega Evolution Special II, it was revealed that he received it from Lysandre. After the Team Flare crisis, Alain gave his Mega Ring to the authorities to be used as evidence and resolved to find himself a new Key Stone on his own. In Curtain Up! Fight the Fights!, Alain was revealed to have obtained a new Mega Ring. In The Visionary, Calem and Serena were seen wearing a Mega Ring each. Neither of them was used during the episode. In Pokémon Horizons: The Series, Mega Rings share their design with those from Pokémon Legends: Z-A. In Where the Adventure Leads, Roy was revealed to be in possession of a Mega Ring. In Eyes to the Skies Once Again (Part One), it was revealed that both Roy and his rival Ult gained their Mega Rings during their training together in the Kalos region. In Mega Evolution - Roy, they were shown to have received their Mega Rings from Master Mayonnaise. Roy uses his Mega Ring to Mega Evolve his Lucario, while Ult uses his to Mega Evolve his Sableye and Dragonite. In Mega Evolution - Ult, Emma used a Mega Ring to Mega Evolve her Ampharos. In HZ122, Friede was revealed to have obtained a Mega Ring during the year he spent apart from the Rising Volt Tacklers. He can use it to Mega Evolve his Starmie and Charizard. In HZ130, Chalce was revealed to possess a Mega Ring. She uses it to Mega Evolve her Medicham. In HZ131, Master Mayonnaise used a Mega Ring to Mega Evolve his Hawlucha during his battle against Roy. X was given a Mega Ring with a Key Stone inside by Gurkinn as a child. With it, he can Mega Evolve his Li'l Kanga, Salamè, Élec, Garma, and Rute, into Mega Kangaskhan, Mega Charizard X, Mega Manectric, Mega Gengar, and Mega Pinsir, respectively. In Pinsir Glares, Y was made a Mega Evolution successor by Gurkinn and given a Mega Ring outfitted with the Key Stone from Gurkinn's Mega Glove. With it, she can Mega Evolve her Solsol into Mega Absol. Pocket Monsters XY: The Legend of the Pokémon Dragon King Taichi owns a Mega Ring, which allows him to Mega Evolve his Ampharos into Mega Ampharos. Lysandre's Key Stone accessory is also known as a Mega Ring; however, it is a finger ring, not a wrist ring. Grisham and Griselle also wear similar rings embedded with their Key Stones, seemingly in imitation of Lysandre.

Title: Power Plant Pass

The Power Plant Pass (Japanese: はつでんしょパス Power Plant Pass) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. The Power Plant Pass allows the player to enter the Kalos Power Plant.

Title: Roller Skates

Roller Skate redirects here. For the Trainer class, see Roller Skater (Trainer class). The Roller Skates (Japanese: ローラースケート Roller Skates) are a Key Item in Pokémon X and Y that allow the player to move around faster than they would by running, but slower than they would on a Bicycle, by navigating with the Nintendo 3DS's Circle Pad. They are based on inline skates. In Pokémon X and Y, in addition to being an item available to the player, Korrina and the Roller Skater Trainer class are also seen wearing Roller Skates. Using the Nintendo 3DS's Circle Pad causes the player to use the Roller Skates, if possible. Pressing the D-pad while using the Roller Skates will cause the player to take them off. In some areas—such as Route 5, Shalour Gym, Route 13, and Dendemille Town—there are rails which the player can grind across using the Roller Skates. These rails frequently feature dips which will cause the player to fall off unless they are moving fast enough. When skating through tall grass, the player is reduced to walking speed and the Poké Radar may not be used. The player can perform certain tricks while using the Roller Skates. Any trick requiring a ledge cannot be performed until the player has learned it from an NPC; otherwise, the player may perform grounded 360s, parallel swizzles, and drift-and-dashes as soon as they have the Roller Skates. These tricks are aesthetic and have no significant gameplay effect. Any trick being correctly performed will be indicated by a high-pitched whooshing noise. In Pokémon Masters EX, Korrina appears wearing Roller Skates. Korrina was seen wearing Roller Skates during her debut in Mega Revelations!. She also used them in her later appearances. In Origins of Mega Evolution!, she was seen skating through a roller skate obstacle course inside the Shalour Gym while arriving to battle Ash. Roller Skates were first seen in Lucky Lucario Was Here, where they were used by Korrina. She used them to perform several tricks to better her Mega Lucario's movements during her battle against Celosia. Later, she gave Tierno a pair of Roller Skates as a gift. Tierno's Roller Skates eventually ended up getting destroyed in Xerneas Gives during the final battle against Team Flare, when they were melted by Mable's Houndoom's Inferno. The Roller Skates were introduced as an Item card in the Pokémon Trading Card Game during the English XY Series (Japanese XY Era). First released in the Japanese Collection X expansion, it later debuted in English in the XY expansion, with both prints featuring an illustration by 5ban Graphics. This Trainer card allows the player to flip a coin and draw three cards if the result is heads. A miniature of a pair of Roller Skates was included in the Kalos goods set from the Pokémon World Market merchandise line that was made by Re-Ment and exclusive to the Pokémon Center. Korrina mentions her Roller Skates during Pokémon Legends: Z-A's Mega Dimension DLC, calling them a "shtick" that she's had to drop by then.

Title: Sprinklotad

The Sprinklotad (Japanese: ハスボーじょうろ Hassboh Watering Can) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is a watering can fashioned after a Lotad. The Sprinklotad allows the player to water the trees they plant at the Berry fields. A Sprinklotad was briefly seen in Under the Pledging Tree!, where it was one of the items offered to Ash by a market vendor in Coumarine City after Ash had wondered out loud what presents he should buy for an upcoming festival. A Sprinklotad briefly appeared in PT12, being used by a Trainer to shower her Leafeon. The Sprinklotad is the only watering can whose design is not based on a pure Water-type Pokémon; Lotad is a Water/Grass-type Pokémon.

Title: Latiasite

The Latiasite (Japanese: ラティアスナイト Latiasnite) is a type of held item introduced in Generation VI. It is the Mega Stone that allows Latias to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias. If held by a Latias, the Latiasite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Latiasite from Latias. If held by a Latias, the Latiasite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Latias will remain a Mega Latias until the Talent Round ends. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Latiasite allows the player to Mega Evolve Latias. The player can obtain the Latiasite by passing Level 50 on a Mega Latias escalation battle in Weeks 8 and 9 of the event rotation. The Latiasite was also available as a prize from Latias escalation battles during the game's live service period: The Latiasite enables the following Mega Evolution: In Latias Flies, Latios and Latias were revealed to have obtained their respective Mega Stones. The Latiasite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise. Data for the Latiasite and the Latiosite exists within the code of Pokémon X and Y, despite the Mega Stones not having been officially introduced until Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, which allows them to be transferred to and used in X and Y.

Title: Latiosite

The Latiosite (Japanese: ラティオスナイト Latiosnite) is a type of held item introduced in Generation VI. It is the Mega Stone that allows Latios to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios. If held by a Latios, the Latiosite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Latiosite from Latios. If held by a Latios, the Latiosite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Latios will remain a Mega Latios until the Talent Round ends. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Latiosite allows the player to Mega Evolve Latios. The player can obtain the Latiosite by passing Level 50 on a Mega Latios escalation battle in Weeks 4 and 5 of the event rotation. The Latiosite was also available as a prize from Latios escalation battles during the game's live service period: The Latiosite enables the following Mega Evolution: In Latias Flies, Latios and Latias were revealed to have obtained their respective Mega Stones. Using the Latiosite in combination with his Mega Bracelet, Ruby was able to Mega Evolve Latios and soar in the sky. He later Mega Evolved Latios again to battle Zinnia and her Mega Salamence at the Sky Pillar. The Latiosite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise. Data for Latiosite and Latiasite exists within the code of Pokémon X and Y, despite the Mega Stones not being officially introduced until Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, which allows them to be transferred to and used in X and Y.

Title: Makeup Bag

The Makeup Bag (Japanese: コスメポーチ Cosmetics Pouch) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VII. It allows female player characters to change the color of their lips. The Makeup Bag stores lipsticks, which allow the player to change their lip color in fitting rooms. The player can choose from the following lipsticks:

Title: Shalour Sable

The Shalour Sable (Japanese: シャラサブレ Shala Sable) is a type of medicine introduced in Generation VI. It is a local specialty of Shalour City and cures a Pokémon from all non-volatile status conditions and confusion. When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it cures a Pokémon of all non-volatile status conditions (poison, paralysis, sleep, burn, freeze) and confusion. In Pokémon: Magikarp Jump, Shalour Sables can appear as food that can be consumed by Magikarp. They are unlocked at Trainer Rank 48 and cost 15,171 to unlock. In The Fiery Road to Mastership!, James's Morpeko was seen eating Shalour Sables. The Shalour Sable, as depicted in the artwork from Scarlet and Violet, has been featured in a figure of Dedenne from the Pokémon Delicious Adventure figure line by The Pokémon Company International, visible when looking at the figure from the back. Although Shalour Sables are not obtainable in Pokémon X and Y without trading, they are mentioned by a female Tourist standing near the Galette Stand in Lumiose City. The Shalour Sable is the only local specialty not obtainable in its home region in the games.

Title: Devon Parts

The Devon Parts (Japanese: デボンのにもつ Devon Bag) are a Key Item introduced in Generation III. In Pokémon Ruby, Sapphire, and Emerald, they were known as the Devon Goods. The Devon Parts are stolen by a Team Magma Grunt/Team Aqua Grunt in Rustboro City after the player has earned the Stone Badge. After the player has gotten the parts back, Mr. Stone requests that they deliver the goods to Captain Stern themselves. The attaché case contains mechanical parts from the Devon Corporation. Giving the parts to Stern in the Oceanic Museum progresses the main storyline. The Devon Parts were first mentioned in Mowing Down Ludicolo, where Team Aqua succeeded in stealing them from Mr. Stone. After Team Magma later succeeded in stealing the Submarine Explorer 1, the two villainous teams formed a temporary alliance in order to safely reach Seafloor Cavern. When the parts stolen by Team Aqua were combined with the submarine stolen by Team Magma, the submersible became able to reach greater depths than usual, allowing it to reach Seafloor Cavern. In Very Vexing Volbeat, after Amber had succeeded in stealing the Blue Orb from Tabitha and delivering it to Archie, the evil team boss trapped both Amber and Tabitha into the submarine and sent it diving into the ocean depths on autopilot. He then revealed that he had removed the Devon Parts from the submarine, dooming both men trapped inside it to die when the deep sea pressure would break the submarine's hull. Fortunately, the two Admins were saved from certain death by Blaise's Armaldo.

Title: Devon Scuba Gear

The Devon Scuba Gear (Japanese: デボンボンベ Devon Tank) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is one of Devon Corporation's products. The goggles are fashioned after a Marshtomp. The Devon Scuba Gear is worn while underwater, giving the player oxygen.

Title: Magma Suit

The Magma Suit (Japanese: マグマスーツ Magma Suit) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. In Pokémon Omega Ruby, the Magma Suit allows the player to reach Groudon's chamber in the Cave of Origin. It is later used in the climax of the Delta Episode to ride Mega Rayquaza into space and confront Deoxys. The Magma Suit appeared in The Scoop, where it was seen being worn by a Trainer while using Mega Rayquaza to destroy a meteor and subsequently battle a Deoxys. The Magma Suit first appeared in Kyogre Returns, under the possession of the second Tabitha. When Ruby and Sapphire later prepared to ride Mega Rayquaza into space to destroy Grand Meteor Delta in Omega Alpha Adventure 21, Ruby was given the Magma Suit to wear for the journey. For this purpose, the suit was upgraded in several ways, including with a set of Key Stones provided by the Devon Corporation in order to Mega Evolve Rayquaza. A replica of the Magma Suit helmet can be bought from Atticus at the Porto Marinada Market in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

Title: Aqua Suit

The Aqua Suit (Japanese: アクアスーツ Aqua Suit) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. In Pokémon Alpha Sapphire, the Aqua Suit allows the player to reach Kyogre's chamber in the Cave of Origin. It is later used in the climax of the Delta Episode to ride Mega Rayquaza into space and confront Deoxys. The Aqua Suit first appeared in Kyogre Returns, under the possession of the second Shelly. When Ruby and Sapphire later prepared to ride Mega Rayquaza into space to destroy Grand Meteor Delta in Omega Alpha Adventure 21, Sapphire was given the Aqua Suit to wear for the journey. For this purpose, the suit was upgraded in several ways, including with a set of Key Stones provided by the Devon Corporation in order to Mega Evolve Rayquaza, and the Green Orb core in order to control it. A replica of the Aqua Suit helmet can be bought from Atticus at the Porto Marinada Market in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

Title: Mega Bracelet

A Mega Bracelet (Japanese: メガバングル Mega Bangle) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is a Key Stone accessory that enables Mega Evolution. Its counterpart in Pokémon X and Y is the Mega Ring. Steven gives the Mega Bracelet to the player on Southern Island after Latios or Latias, holding its respective Mega Stone, joins the player. The player character also wears a special Mega Bracelet during Pokémon Contest Spectaculars. Brendan's is a Mega Cuff (Japanese: メガブレス Mega Bracelet), a black band with a circle of large, connected red diamonds affixed to it, with the Key Stone embedded in the band; May's is a Mega Charm (Japanese: メガチャーム Mega Charm), a chain of cream bows with the Key Stone embedded into a pink heart charm. After the player has defeated the Champion, their neighbor Brendan/May is revealed to have obtained a Mega Bracelet of their own, which they use in a post-credits battle on Route 103. Secret Base Trainer avatars of player characters that have a Mega Bracelet will use it in battle if possible. Allows the player to Mega Evolve their Pokémon if they hold the appropriate Mega Stone. Each Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle. On August 27, 2020, in conjunction with the release of Mega Evolution in Pokémon GO, the Mega Bracelet was added to the Style Shop. However, unlike the core series games, they are not required to Mega Evolve a Pokémon; rather, they are merely for cosmetic purposes. In The Vision, Brendan was seen wearing his Mega Bracelet, although he was never seen using it. Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire Animated Trailer In the Pokémon Omega Ruby and Pokémon Alpha Sapphire Animated Trailer, Brendan and May were seen wearing their Mega Bracelets. They used them to Mega Evolve the Hoenn first partner Pokémon. May was also briefly seen wearing her Mega Charm during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular while performing with a Mega Altaria and Mega Audino. In The Wish, May was seen wearing her Mega Bracelet. She used it first to Mega Evolve her Swampert and later her newly caught Rayquaza. Ruby, Sapphire, and Emerald acquired Mega Bracelets from Ultima and Steven in preparation for another disaster coming to Hoenn. In Claydol Unleashes, Ruby successfully used his Mega Bracelet and Latios's Latiosite to Mega Evolve the Eon Pokémon and soar with it to the Sky Pillar. The three aforementioned Pokédex holders can also use their Mega Bracelets to Mega Evolve their respective Hoenn first partner Pokémon. In Omega Alpha Adventure 21, Ruby and Sapphire were also seen wearing a Mega Cuff and a Mega Charm, respectively, during a Contest Spectacular. Ruby used his Mega Cuff to Mega Evolve Diancie for his Appeal. Diantha's Key Stone accessory is also known as a Mega Charm; however, it is a golden necklace, not a bracelet.

Title: Swampertite

The Swampertite (Japanese: ラグラージナイト Laglargenite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Swampert to Mega Evolve into Mega Swampert. If held by a Swampert, the Swampertite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Swampert if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Swampertite from Swampert. If held by a Swampert, the Swampertite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Swampert when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Swampert will remain a Mega Swampert until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Swampertite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Swampertite allows the player to Mega Evolve Swampert. The player can obtain the Swampertite by completing Mission Card 18. The Swampertite was also available as a prize during the Mega Swampert competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Swampertite enables the following Mega Evolution: In Claydol Unleashes, it was revealed that Steven had found a Swampertite for Ruby's Swampert, Mumu. The Swampertite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

The Sceptilite was also available as a prize during the Mega Sceptile competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Sceptilite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Sceptilite first appeared in Valuable Experience for All!. It was worn by Sawyer's Sceptile on a bandanna that is tied around its neck. In Claydol Unleashes, it was revealed that Steven had found a Sceptilite for Emerald's Sceptile. Title: Sablenite

The Sablenite (Japanese: ヤミラミナイト Yamiraminite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Sableye to Mega Evolve into Mega Sableye. If held by a Sableye, the Sablenite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Sableye if its Trainer has a Key Stone. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Sablenite allows the player to Mega Evolve Sableye and Spooky Sableye. The player can obtain the Sablenite by defeating Mega Sableye in Stage 30 found on Night Festival. The Sablenite enables the following Mega Evolutions: A Sablenite debuted in Eyes to the Skies Once Again (Part One), under the ownership of Ult, who uses it to Mega Evolve his Sableye. In Mega Evolution - Roy, it was revealed that Ult found it in Lumiose City while picking up garbage.

Title: Altarianite

The Altarianite (Japanese: チルタリスナイト Tyltalisnite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Altaria to Mega Evolve into Mega Altaria. If held by an Altaria, the Altarianite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Altaria if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove an Altarianite from Altaria. If held by an Altaria, allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Altaria when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular while its Trainer has a Key Stone. Altaria will remain a Mega Altaria until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding an Altarianite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Altarianite allows the player to Mega Evolve Altaria. The player can obtain the Altarianite by defeating Mega Altaria in Stage 75 found on Galerie Rouge. The Altarianite enables the following Mega Evolution: An Altarianite belonging to Drasna appeared in Taking Two For The Team!. She used it to Mega Evolve her Altaria during her World Coronation Series battle against Ash, but despite the power-up, Altaria was still defeated. Another Altarianite appeared in Reuniting for the First Time!, under the ownership of Lisia. She used it to Mega Evolve her Altaria, Ali, during the Lilycove City Pokémon Contest Spectacular. The Altarianite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Galladite

If held by a Gallade, the Galladite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Gallade if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). If held by a Gallade, the Galladite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Gallade when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Galladite allows the player to Mega Evolve Gallade. The player can obtain the Galladite by defeating Mega Gallade in Stage 580 found on Prasino Woods. The Galladite enables the following Mega Evolution: In PS616, the Meteor Village elder offered Sapphire a Gardevoirite and Galladite, commenting on how her Gallade and Ruby's Gardevoir are a match. Ruby and Sapphire later used these Mega Stones to Mega Evolve Rara and Kirly during a brief battle against each other.

Title: Audinite

The Audinite (Japanese: タブンネナイト Tabunnenite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Audino to Mega Evolve into Mega Audino. If held by an Audino, the Audinite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Audino if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove an Audinite from Audino. If held by an Audino, the Audinite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Audino when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Audino will remain a Mega Audino until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding an Audinite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Audinite allows the player to Mega Evolve Audino and Winking Audino to Mega Audino. The player can obtain the Audinite by defeating Mega Audino in Stage 10, the final stage of Puerto Blanco. Its Trainer gives the player the Mega Stone as a reward for winning. The Audinite enables the following Mega Evolutions: Having the Audinite allows the player to view Audino and Winking Audino from the Pokémon List even if they are not captured, though their icons are faded and their type, attack power, and Skill are not visible. However, Stage 9 is guaranteed to conclude with Audino being captured, so in practice this can only apply to Winking Audino. An Audinite appeared in A Giga Battle with Mega Results!, where a Nurse Joy owning an Audino had found one during her efforts to mend their recently-souring relationship. She later used it and Professor Sycamore's Key Stone to Mega Evolve Audino in order to fight against Team Rocket. The Audinite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Metagrossite

It is the Mega Stone that allows Metagross to Mega Evolve into Mega Metagross. If held by a Metagross, the Metagrossite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Metagross if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Metagrossite from Metagross. Metagross will remain a Mega Metagross until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Metagrossite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Metagrossite allows the player to Mega Evolve Metagross and Shiny Metagross. The player can obtain the Metagrossite by defeating Mega Metagross in Stage 500 found on Graucus Hall. The Metagrossite enables the following Mega Evolutions: Steven's Shiny Metagross's Metagrossite debuted in Mega Evolution Special II, where it was worn as a large bracelet on its right foreleg. In Hoopa Distorts, Steven was revealed to be in possession of a Metagrossite. The Metagrossite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Slowbronite

The Slowbronite (Japanese: ヤドランナイト Yadorannite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Kantonian Slowbro to Mega Evolve into Mega Slowbro. If held by a Kantonian Slowbro (or is present in the player's Bag), the Slowbronite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Slowbro if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Slowbronite from Slowbro. If held by a Slowbro, the Slowbronite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Slowbro when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Slowbro will remain a Mega Slowbro until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Slowbronite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Slowbronite allows the player to Mega Evolve Slowbro. The player can obtain the Slowbronite by defeating Mega Slowbro in Stage 45 found on Isla Asul. The Slowbronite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Slowbronite appeared during Gurkinn's explanation about Mega Evolution in An Adventure of Mega Proportions!. In Slowbro Has an Idea, Jinga was revealed to possess a Slowbronite, which he used to Mega Evolve his Slowbro. The Slowbronite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Title: Steelixite

The Steelixite (Japanese: ハガネールナイト Haganeilnite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Steelix to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix. If held by a Steelix, the Steelixite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Steelixite from Steelix. If held by a Steelix, the Steelixite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Steelix will remain a Mega Steelix until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Steelixite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Steelixite allows the player to Mega Evolve Steelix. The player can obtain the Steelixite by completing the competitive stage available in Week 5 of the event rotation The Steelixite was also available as a prize during the Mega Steelix competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Steelixite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Steelixite worn by Brock's Steelix appeared in When Regions Collide!, where Brock used it to Mega Evolve Steelix during his battle with Kiawe. The Steelixite didn't appear equipped on Steelix, but rather, it appeared on its head right before Mega Evolving.

Title: Pidgeotite

The Pidgeotite (Japanese: ピジョットナイト Pigeotnite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Pidgeot to Mega Evolve into Mega Pidgeot. If held by a Pidgeot (or is present in the player's Bag), the Pidgeotite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Pidgeot if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Pidgeotite from Pidgeot. If held by a Pidgeot, the Pidgeotite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Pidgeot when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Pidgeot will remain a Mega Pidgeot until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Pidgeotite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Pidgeotite allows the player to Mega Evolve Pidgeot. The player can obtain the Pidgeotite by reaching Trainer rank 12. The Pidgeotite was also available as a prize during the Mega Pidgeot competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Pidgeotite enables the following Mega Evolutions: The Pidgeotite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

If held by a Glalie, the Glalitite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Glalie when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Title: Diancite

The Diancite (Japanese: ディアンシナイト Diancinite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Diancie to Mega Evolve into Mega Diancie. If held by a Diancie, the Diancite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Diancie if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Diancite from Diancie. If held by a Diancie, the Diancite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Diancie when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Diancie will remain a Mega Diancie until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Diancite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Diancite allows the player to Mega Evolve Diancie and Shiny Diancie. The player can obtain the Diancite by passing Level 50 on a Mega Diancie escalation battle in Weeks 10 and 11 of the event rotation. The Diancite was also available as a prize from Diancie escalation battles during the game's live service period: August 22 to September 5, 2017 (3DS and Mobile) (Boss was Shiny version) December 12 to 26, 2017 (3DS and Mobile) (Boss was Shiny version) The Diancite enables the following Mega Evolutions: In Omega Alpha Adventure 21, Ruby used Hoopa's ring to find a Diancite for Diancie to use in a Pokémon Contest Spectacular. This allowed him to Mega Evolve Diancie for his and Sapphire's performance.

Title: Prison Bottle

The Prison Bottle (Japanese: いましめのツボ Jar of Binding) is a Key Item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is used to transform Hoopa Confined to Hoopa Unbound. In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, the Prison Bottle can be obtained through having Hoopa in the party and entering a Poké Mart. A new clerk will appear near the counter at the far left side. If the player interacts with this clerk, the game transitions to another view as the clerk tells the player a story. After his story, he will give the player the Prison Bottle. "Hey, do you have a minute for a story? This is something that happened when I was watching the store one time... It was, like, a totally dark night. Moonless. And I was all, "No way! This is so-o-o-o creepy!" Then, suddenly—whoosh! Out of nowhere, the automatic sliding door...slides open. And then this super-duper-scary old guy comes in, kinda unsteady, you know? Kinda shambling... So I'm thinking how terrible and scary is this, and then the old guy shuffles over to me... He has a package, like, clutched in his hands... He's mumbling that he has to show it to me... Then he asks, "Do you know what this is worth?" He keeps staring right at me and not even blinking. I was so freaked out. I was, like... "Oh yes. It's, uh, really something, isn't it?" So the old guy grins at me, and he says... "Then I'll just give you this instead of money. Now give me everything you've got in this store!" That's what he said! Really! I was like, "What is this? Too weird, my dude!" But I was scared, so I started shoveling over Potions and, like, whatever I could reach. I gave the dude everything for his stupid package. And out he goes, like, with a wicked grin on his face... As he's walking out, he says, "Three days... For a mere three days will Hoopa be unbound..." Really! I was all, "Stop freaking me out with this nonsense!" I was, like, sweating. The back of my shirt was soaked. Anyway, nothing I could do, so I open the package. It's just a dirty old bottle. Li-i-i-i-ike, really. So I go, "Weirdness. Wonder what's in it." I grab the stopper and carefully pull it loose. Blapt! It popped open, but there was nothing inside! So I figure I totally got fooled. ...Thanks for listening to my story. Know what? I'm gonna give you the bottle from that night 'cause you're so nice. It's not doing me any good anyway. Really." "Sometimes I wonder if it really is only a dirty old bottle. Those last words that the old guy said as he left... "For a mere three days will Hoopa be unbound..." Just can't seem to get that out of my head..." "Oh... You're that kid from that time... What did you end up doing with that old bottle? Though...I guess it doesn't matter anymore. Really. It's just, even now, it's still bugging me, you know? "For a mere three days will Hoopa be unbound..." That's what he said. I just can't seem to forget it..." When used from the Bag on a Hoopa Confined, it changes into Hoopa Unbound for three days. Hoopa Unbound also changes back to Hoopa Confined when deposited into the PC (prior to Generation VII), when withdrawn from the PC (Generation VII), when deposited into Pokémon Bank, or when deposited in Pokémon Day Care or the Pokémon Nursery. The Prison Bottle played a major role in the movie Hoopa and the Clash of Ages. One hundred years prior to the events of the movie, Hoopa Unbound caused great calamity in Dahara City with its battles against multiple Legendary Pokémon. Using the power of Arceus's Splash, Flame, and Earth Plates, a man named Ghris created the Prison Bottle, which he used to seal away Hoopa's Unbound power, before hiding it away. In the present time, Ghris's great-grandson, Baraz, found the bottle and unsealed it. However, upon touching the bottle, he was possessed by the dark power sealed inside. He then returned to Dahara City and opened the bottle, causing Hoopa to transform back into Hoopa Unbound. This caused Hoopa to go on a rampage once again, prompting Meray to reseal the Unbound power back into the bottle. The bottle was later stolen by Team Rocket, who wanted to get their hands on the power inside of it. This action allowed Hoopa Unbound to possess Meowth and force it to release it once again. With the help of Baraz and Meray, Hoopa was able to resist the dark power from taking over its body. However, the bottle then unexpectedly shattered into pieces, and the disembodied dark power formed into its own entity, a "shadow" of Hoopa. Baraz realized that the shadow was formed out of Hoopa's anger of being imprisoned for a hundred years, and it had destroyed the bottle to avoid being sealed away again. Knowing that the Prison Bottle could be remade, Baraz guided Meray, Clemont, Serena, and Bonnie to Dahara Tower, where they used Frogadier's Water Pulse, Braixen's Flamethrower, and a wild Hippopotas's Sand Attack to provide the same elements that had been used to create the bottle a hundred years earlier: Water, Fire, and Earth. The Prison Bottle was successfully recreated, and Hoopa's shadow was sealed inside it. Although the shadow temporarily took over Ash, Hoopa was able to drive away its dark power. This allowed Ash to use the bottle to grant Hoopa the power of Hoopa Unbound in order to save the people of Dahara City from a disturbance in space-time without losing control of itself. Afterwards, Hoopa was peacefully returned to Hoopa Confined. During the movie's ending credits, Meray was seen transforming Hoopa into Hoopa Unbound so that it could help rebuild the destroyed Dahara Tower. The Prison Bottle appeared in Hoopa and the Clash of Ages, fulfilling the same role as in the movie.

Title: Cameruptite

The Cameruptite (Japanese: バクーダナイト Bakuudanite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Camerupt to Mega Evolve into Mega Camerupt. If held by a Camerupt, the Cameruptite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Camerupt if its Trainer has a Key Stone. A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment). As a Mega Stone, it is unaffected by item-negating effects (such as Embargo and Klutz). Item-manipulating effects (such as Trick and Fling) cannot give or remove a Cameruptite from Camerupt. If held by a Camerupt, the Cameruptite allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Camerupt when it performs a Spectacular Talent during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular if its Trainer has a Key Stone. Camerupt will remain a Mega Camerupt until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Cameruptite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. In Pokémon Shuffle, the Cameruptite allows the player to Mega Evolve Camerupt. The player can obtain the Cameruptite by completing the competitive stage available in Week 15 of the event rotation. The Cameruptite was also available as a prize during the Mega Camerupt competitive stage during the game's live service period: The Cameruptite enables the following Mega Evolution: A Cameruptite appeared during Gurkinn's explanation about Mega Evolution in An Adventure of Mega Proportions!. In Blastoise Transforms, Maxie was revealed to have obtained a Cameruptite. After his apparent death, Blaise took possession of the Mega Stone. The Cameruptite has been featured in several officially licensed merchandise.

Lopunny will remain a Mega Lopunny until the Talent Round ends. Any Pokémon holding a Lopunnite in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire cannot be traded to Pokémon X and Y. Title: Beedrillite

The Beedrillite (Japanese: スピアナイト Speanite) is a type of held item introduced in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the Mega Stone that allows Beedrill to Mega Evolve into Mega Beedrill.
